projects.title: Safety First Training
projects.subtitle: Can you identify workplace hazards before they become incidents?
projects.description: This scenario-based eLearning project prepares new employees to identify and respond to common workplace safety hazards through realistic, branching scenarios with meaningful consequences.
projects.overview: This scenario-based eLearning course was designed for a manufacturing company to train new employees on workplace safety protocols. Learners navigate through realistic workplace scenarios where they must identify hazards and make critical safety decisions.
projects.problem: New employees were struggling to apply safety protocols in real-world situations, leading to a 15% increase in minor incidents during the first 90 days.
projects.solution: I designed an interactive, scenario-based eLearning module that places learners in realistic workplace situations. Using branching scenarios, learners experience the consequences of their safety decisions in a risk-free environment.
projects.results: After implementation, workplace incidents among new employees decreased by 40% in the first quarter.
process: Conducted needs analysis with safety managers and observed workplace conditions
Created action map identifying critical safety behaviors
Developed storyboard with branching scenario paths
Built interactive prototype in Articulate Storyline
Conducted usability testing with sample audience
Revised based on feedback and deployed via LMS

projects.title: Welcome Aboard: New Hire Orientation
projects.subtitle: Everything new team members need to hit the ground running
projects.description: This comprehensive onboarding course combines self-paced eLearning modules with interactive activities to help new hires understand company culture, policies, and their role responsibilities.
projects.overview: A comprehensive onboarding experience designed for a mid-size tech company, combining self-paced learning modules with interactive knowledge checks and real-world application activities.
projects.problem: The existing onboarding process relied on lengthy PDFs and live presentations, resulting in low engagement and inconsistent knowledge transfer across departments.
projects.solution: I redesigned the onboarding experience as a blended learning program, combining Articulate Rise modules for self-paced learning with Camtasia video walkthroughs for software training.
projects.results: New hire satisfaction scores increased by 35%, and time-to-productivity decreased by 2 weeks on average.
process: Interviewed stakeholders and new hires to identify pain points
Mapped learning objectives to measurable outcomes
Designed microlearning modules in Articulate Rise
Developed interactive job aids and quick-reference guides
Piloted with a cohort of 15 new hires and iterated

projects.title: Quick Reference: Customer Service Excellence
projects.subtitle: A visual guide to handling customer interactions with confidence
projects.description: This performance-based job aid provides customer service representatives with a visual, step-by-step guide to handling common customer scenarios, from greeting to resolution.
projects.overview: A visually engaging job aid designed for retail customer service representatives, providing quick-reference guidance for common customer interaction scenarios.
projects.problem: Customer service reps frequently consulted lengthy policy manuals during interactions, leading to longer resolution times and decreased customer satisfaction.
projects.solution: I created a visual, scannable job aid that distills key procedures into an intuitive flowchart format with clear decision points and scripted responses.
projects.results: Average call resolution time decreased by 20%, and customer satisfaction scores improved by 12%.
process: Observed customer service interactions and identified common scenarios
Interviewed top-performing reps for best practices
Designed visual flowchart with decision points
Created polished graphics in Canva and Adobe Illustrator
Tested with a pilot group and refined based on feedback
Distributed digitally and as printed desk reference
